steps.title: Spot the contradiction
steps.content:
The first sentence states scientists isolated many elements quickly.

The second sentence, starting with the blank, says “this wasn't the case with all elements.” The word “wasn't” directly contradicts the previous claim, signaling that the transition must acknowledge one fact while introducing a contrasting one.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the transition
steps.content:
”Granted,” is used when you acknowledge one fact but then present a contrasting or exceptional case—here, you accept “scientists isolated many elements quickly” but then explain “this wasn't true for all elements.” The other three options don't fit this logic: they suggest consequence in the wrong direction, similarity where the text shows difference, or restatement instead of exception.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
